title: Ineffable Space Time
definition: The inability to fully comprehend the true nature of space-time due to the limitations of language and human perception.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The experience of time and space cannot be accurately explained or described using words, as it transcends our understanding and lies beyond the reach of language.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The complexity and ambiguity of space-time (as described by General Relativity) make it difficult to grasp with conventional methods of understanding and communication.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Qualia Problem in philosophy of mind suggests that our concepts and language are insufficient for capturing the full richness of lived experiences like a shift in time or the feeling of being in a specific space.

extra_fields.insight: Space-time, as described by physics, is fundamentally ineffable due to its complexity and the limitations of human perception and understanding.
